memoryArtifacts.headline: Barony of Tyeys
memoryArtifacts.description:
Sir Henry Tyeys, 1st Baron of Chilton Foliot, son and heir, was a minor in 1284, when he had succeeded to the Foliot Estates.  His mother was Joan Foliot.

Sir Henry served in Wales in 1287 and 1292 a …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry de Tyeys於大約1268年出生在英國奧可福爾福利特維爾時，他的父親Henry de Tyeys35歲，母親Joan Foliot33歲。他於1284年與Hawise Montagu在英國英格蘭奧可福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1307年10月8日在英國維爾爾爾頓 福利亞特享年40歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾爾頓 福利亞特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Henry de Tyeys于大约1268年出生在英国奧可福爾福利特維爾时，他的父亲Henry de Tyeys35岁，母亲Joan Foliot33岁。他于1284年与Hawise Montagu在英国英格兰奧可福爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1307年10月8日在英国維爾爾爾頓 福利亞特享年40岁，埋葬在英国維爾爾爾頓 福利亞特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tyeys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Camille, Denys, Emile, Philippe.
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Theijs, from a short form of the personal name Mathijs, Dutch form of Matthew .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry de Tyeys was born about 1268, in Fritwell, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Henry de Tyeys, was 35 and his mother, Joan Foliot, was 33. He married Hawise Montagu in 1284, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 8 October 1307, in Chilton Foliat, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Chilton Foliat, Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry de Tyeys est né environ 1268, à Fritwell, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Tyeys, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Joan Foliot, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Hawise Montagu en 1284, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 8 octobre 1307, à Chilton Foliat, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Chilton Foliat, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Henry de Tyeys um 1268 in Fritwell, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry de Tyeys 35 und seine Mutter Joan Foliot 33. Er hat 1284 in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom Hawise Montagu geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 8. Lui sposato Hawise Montagu nel 1284, nel Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Tyeysが約1268年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアフリトウエルで生まれた時，父Henry de Tyeysは35歳，母Joan Foliotは33歳であった。1284年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアでHawise Montaguと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1307年10月8日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアチルトン フォリアトで40歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアチルトン フォリアトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Tyeys(이)가 대략 1268년 영국 스퍼드실르 프릿웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Tyeys(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Foliot(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1284년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 Hawise Montagu(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1307년 10월 8일 영국 윌츠히르 치턴 폴리앳에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 치턴 폴리앳에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Hawise Montagu em 1284, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry de Tyeys родился около 1268 г. в местности фритвелл, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry de Tyeys было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Foliot, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise Montagu в 1284 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 8 октября 1307 г. в местности чилтон фолят, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности чилтон фолят, уилцхир, Англия.
